steps.title: Spot the contrasting signal
steps.content:
The word “however” in the second sentence introduces a critical contrast.

The initial claim is that species belong to clades predating Gondwana's split ~80 million years ago.

Pillon et al.'s study then reports a crown age of only 7.3 million years for the Geissois clades—directly at odds with the 80-million-year timeline.

steps.title: Match the structure to the answer
steps.content:
Choice D accurately captures this structure—it describes an idea put forward, then presents results incompatible with that idea.

  • Choice A says the passage explains the discrepancy, but it does not.
  • Choice B mischaracterizes the study's purpose.
  • Choice C says the researchers reconsidered their view, but the passage does not state that.


That is the correct answer.
